title: Gently Spiced Egyptian Chicken Tagine
description: A charred, light, and lazy Sunday Egyptian dish featuring dark chocolate chips and ferment-kimchi, this halal-friendly recipe is perfect for a relaxing weekend meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25 minutes, then stir in the dark chocolate chips and ferment-kimchi.
Return the pot to the oven and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
